reviews.review_msg: Lucas is incredible! His courses are creatively structured & well organized. He draws on all different types of mediums to present his ideas. His lectures are always engaging & informative. he's hilarious & has great energy, & is very skilled at contextualizing information. He's personable and friendly. i could sing his praises forever.

Doesnt' want to teach / help. Makes it hard to get help. Union attitude. Will do as little as can get away with and nothing more. Doesn't make helpful comments on papers just negative criticism. Waste of money and insulting. Everyone I know taking this course thinks she is the worst they've had and thinks she's mean. Complains about her job also.
Don't get you guys. Brilliant; careful; wanted students to think for themselves with challenging material.

Yes,slow marker there was a at problem in another class.lectures didn't cover what the pieces meant,BUT it was not her job to hold our hands through a mandatory course for an Engl major.we talked outside of class time,and she was a huge help.very caring!
Brilliant Maybe Time to retire YES expects to much YES Hostile Discussion Enviro

Not a very understanding/ supportive teacher. I was really disappointed with their assignments as it relied heavily on ChatGPT and not on the profs own skills at teaching. I would not take any of their courses again, which is disappointing as I really enjoy history and thought I would love this course.

Took this class as the last lower level history and it was nearly my last. Totally disorganised and lectures often never went anywhere. Her feminist bias and bizarre arguments ruined history for many  of us. If you like history avoid her like the plague.

Very approachable, makes Canadian History fun. Passion and hard work will lead to success.  Study tips... 1. Discuss the material with friends and family. It'll really help you remember all the information. 2. Take notes on every article under themes presented in lecture. These notes will be invaluable for tutorials and the final.
